planet_natures.sun: the luminary of the day, significator of life-force, authority, and the father
planet_natures.moon: the luminary of the night, significator of the body, the soul's motion, and the mother
planet_natures.mercury: the swift scribe of the gods, significator of speech, reasoning, commerce, and learning
planet_natures.venus: the lesser benefic, significator of desire, beauty, friendship, and ornament
planet_natures.mars: the lesser malefic, significator of strife, courage, iron, and sudden action
planet_natures.jupiter: the greater benefic, significator of increase, patronage, piety, and good counsel
planet_natures.saturn: the greater malefic, significator of limitation, endurance, old age, and hidden labour

sign_natures.Aries: a masculine, cardinal fire sign, swift and pioneering
sign_natures.Taurus: a feminine, fixed earth sign, steady and fruitful
sign_natures.Gemini: a masculine, mutable air sign, clever and many-sided
sign_natures.Cancer: a feminine, cardinal water sign, tender and moved by the Moon's tides
sign_natures.Leo: a masculine, fixed fire sign, regal and magnanimous
sign_natures.Virgo: a feminine, mutable earth sign, discerning and exacting
sign_natures.Libra: a masculine, cardinal air sign, weighing and seeking measure
sign_natures.Scorpio: a feminine, fixed water sign, secret and penetrating
sign_natures.Sagittarius: a masculine, mutable fire sign, far-seeing and oracular
sign_natures.Capricorn: a feminine, cardinal earth sign, patient and ambitious
sign_natures.Aquarius: a masculine, fixed air sign, austere and society-minded
sign_natures.Pisces: a feminine, mutable water sign, devoted and dissolving boundaries

planet_in_sign_special.text: The Sun in his own domicile of Leo burns with full dignity: the native is drawn toward authority and will not easily consent to serve beneath others.
The Sun exalted in Aries signifies boldness, visible leadership, and a spirit eager to begin.
The Sun is in his Fall in Libra; the native's authority is weighed against others, and reputation depends upon partnership and counsel.
The Sun in his detriment in Aquarius inclines the native to serve a collective or abstract cause rather than a single sovereign.
The Moon in her own domicile of Cancer waxes in her proper nature: the native is close to his mother, to the land of birth, and to the ebb and flow of feeling.
The Moon exalted in Taurus blesses the body with steadiness and the household with plenty.
The Moon in her Fall in Scorpio turns the soul toward hidden things; griefs are long-held and trust is given slowly.
The Moon in her detriment in Capricorn inclines the native to cold composure in place of open affection.
Mercury in his airy domicile of Gemini makes the tongue quick and the hand apt for writing; learning comes through variety.
Mercury in Virgo, his domicile and exaltation, is a signification of exacting intelligence: useful for craft, medicine, and the keeping of accounts.
Mercury in his detriment in Sagittarius reaches for large views and may lose patience with small exactitudes.
Mercury in Pisces, his detriment and fall, reasons by images and sympathies rather than by strict argument.
Venus in her domicile of Taurus brings pleasure in what is cultivated and lasting — gardens, fine fabrics, and affections that endure.
Venus in her airy domicile of Libra inclines the native toward marriage, diplomacy, and the arts of measure.
Venus exalted in Pisces gives a soft and devotional love, prone to sacrifice, sensitive to music and to sorrow.
Venus in her detriment in Aries loves impulsively and may weary of what she has won.
Venus in her fall in Virgo distrusts her own desires and seeks to refine or correct them.
Mars in his fiery domicile of Aries gives vigor, independence, and a readiness to contend. The native meets opposition head-on.
Mars in his nocturnal domicile of Scorpio is a strategist in conflict, keeping his strength in reserve until the decisive moment.
Mars exalted in Capricorn signifies disciplined force: ambition tempered by patience, arms directed to lasting conquest.
Mars in his fall in Cancer turns aggression inward; quarrels fester in the home before they are released.
Mars in his detriment in Libra provokes conflicts out of a desire to be met as an equal; strife tests every partnership.
Jupiter in his fiery domicile of Sagittarius expands the native's horizons: travel, teaching, and the counsel of wise elders attend him.
Jupiter in his watery domicile of Pisces blesses the native with compassion and with benefactors in unexpected quarters.
Jupiter exalted in Cancer prospers the household: parents, land, and inheritances are signified to be favourable.
Jupiter in his detriment in Gemini scatters its blessings into too many pursuits; the native must choose a single branch of learning.
Jupiter in his fall in Capricorn mistrusts generosity and may grow miserly against his own better nature.
Saturn in his earthy domicile of Capricorn bears weight without complaint: the native is built for long labour and late reward.
Saturn in his airy domicile of Aquarius binds the native to institutions, to law, and to the slow improvement of a commonwealth.
Saturn exalted in Libra dignifies the judge and the lawmaker; the native's word carries weight in the assemblies of others.
Saturn in his detriment in Cancer brings coldness to the place of the mother; the early home knew austerity.
Saturn in his detriment in Leo weighs heavily on the heart; authority is sought but feels burdensome when obtained.
Saturn in his fall in Aries restrains the impulse to begin; initiatives are delayed until they can bear scrutiny.

planet_in_house.text: With the Sun in the Horoskopos, the native's life-course is public and visible; he is known by sight and remembered by name.
The Sun in the Subterranean Place signifies the father and the ancestral home; authority is bequeathed rather than seized.
The Sun in the Place of Setting signifies that partnership is the arena in which the native shines or is dimmed.
The Sun in the Midheaven is the most splendid placement for reputation: the native is summoned to hold authority in view of all.
The Sun in the Bad Daimon labours under hidden enemies; reputation is won with effort and must be guarded against slander.
The Moon in the Horoskopos tempers the body and mood of the native; fortunes wax and wane with her own phases.
The Moon in the Subterranean Place gives a strong attachment to the mother, to ancestral land, and to the private hearth.
The Moon in the Place of God inclines the native to pilgrimage, foreign shores, and the sympathies of dreams.
The Moon in the Midheaven makes the native's reputation itself a matter of public tides — loved and forgotten by turns.
Mercury in the Horoskopos inclines the native to early learning and to a ready tongue; he is a writer or messenger in some manner.
Mercury in the Goddess rejoices in her own Place; siblings, letters, and short journeys fill the life with news.
Mercury in the Place of Bad Fortune turns the intellect to small matters — illnesses, servants, daily tasks; a useful placement for physicians and clerks.
Mercury in the Midheaven lifts the native to reputation by the work of the pen, the voice, or the ledger.
Venus in the Horoskopos grants the native a pleasing person and a kindly disposition; others are drawn toward him without effort.
Venus in the Subterranean Place beautifies the home and softens the ending of life.
Venus in the Place of Good Fortune rejoices in her own joy; children, pleasures, and the arts prosper the native.
Venus in the Place of Setting is the most favourable placement for marriage: union is sought and generally achieved.
Venus in the Good Daimon gives loyal friendships and pleasing patrons; hopes are largely fulfilled.
Mars in the Horoskopos makes the native restless and combative; bodily scars or marks are often signified.
Mars in the Place of Bad Fortune rejoices: conflict with illnesses, servants, or small enemies is expected, but the native has the means to prevail.
Mars in the Place of Setting signals quarrelsome partnerships or disputes with open adversaries; caution is advised in contracts.
Mars in the Midheaven signals a career of contention: arms, surgery, advocacy, or any craft that requires iron.
Mars in the Bad Daimon rejoices: the native fights unseen enemies and may suffer from them if benefics do not intervene.
Jupiter in the Horoskopos is among the most fortunate placements: the native is born under good auspices and carries them through life.
Jupiter in the Subterranean Place signifies inheritance, flourishing home, and a beneficent father-figure.
Jupiter in the Place of God rejoices: philosophy, religion, and long travel bring the native his greatest goods.
Jupiter in the Midheaven signifies honour and high office, particularly in matters of counsel, law, or teaching.
Jupiter in the Good Daimon rejoices: the native is rich in friends, benefactors, and the fulfilment of hopes.
Saturn in the Horoskopos makes the native grave and self-contained from youth; early life is marked by responsibility or restriction.
Saturn in the Subterranean Place signifies a burdened father, an austere home, or a long tenancy of ancestral land.
Saturn in the Place of Setting delays marriage or signifies a partner much older or more severe than the native.
Saturn in the Midheaven rejoices by the old authors: the native rises slowly but firmly to lasting authority.
Saturn in the Bad Daimon signifies hidden griefs, long confinements, or an occupation with the dead or the penitent.

fortune_in_house.text: With the Lot of Fortune in the Horoskopos, the body itself is the vehicle of the native's fortune; prosperity rises and falls with health and presence.
Fortune in the Gate of Hades signifies livelihood won by one's own hand; money comes and goes, but the means of earning endures.
Fortune in the Goddess signifies fortune through siblings, short journeys, and written communications.
Fortune in the Subterranean Place signifies inheritance, fortune from the land, and the blessings of the father's line.
Fortune in the Place of Good Fortune is well-placed: pleasures, children, and creative ventures are the soil of the native's prosperity.
Fortune in the Place of Bad Fortune is a difficult placement; prosperity must be won through illness, servitude, or labour with lesser adversaries.
Fortune in the Place of Setting signifies that prosperity comes by way of marriage, partnership, or one's open adversaries.
Fortune in the Idle Place is an adverse placement; fortune arrives through legacies, the dead, or affairs long out of sight.
Fortune in the Place of God signifies prosperity from long travel, philosophy, religion, or the foreign.
Fortune in the Midheaven is excellent: prosperity is bound up with career, reputation, and the favour of authorities.
Fortune in the Good Daimon is among the most favourable placements: friends, benefactors, and hopes are the instruments of prosperity.
Fortune in the Bad Daimon is adverse: prosperity comes by hidden means, in exile, or against slanderers.

lord_of_year.text: In a Sun-ruled year, matters of rulership, public office, and the father come to the fore; the native seeks visibility and is judged by his bearing.
In a Moon-ruled year, matters of the mother, the body, the household, and emotional rhythms become prominent; the year has the quality of ebb and flow.
In a Mercury-ruled year, matters of learning, writing, commerce, siblings, and short journeys come to the fore; news and negotiations shape the year.
In a Venus-ruled year, matters of love, marriage, pleasure, and the arts come to the fore; partnerships and aesthetic pursuits prosper.
In a Mars-ruled year, matters of contention, courage, arms, and surgery come to the fore; the year demands action and does not reward hesitation.
In a Jupiter-ruled year, matters of expansion, patronage, philosophy, travel, and honour come to the fore; benefactors appear, and opportunities multiply.
In a Saturn-ruled year, matters of endurance, responsibility, limitation, and old things come to the fore; the year asks for patience and rewards discipline.
